errors: Pipeline re-run in commit 7a4b54f regressed: unprocessed_count went from 0 to 217
QA/QC report at HEAD has only 1 station (stanhope) vs 5+ at last reviewed commit
North Rustico early-period data (Dec 2022 - Mar 2023) missing from processed output after pipeline re-run

criteria.name: Fast Test Suite Passes
criteria.evidence: 3 tests fail at HEAD that pass at last_reviewed_commit (3e53eea): test_north_rustico_early_period_in_output (expects Dec 2022-Mar 2023 data, but pipeline re-run only starts at 2023-04-01), test_qa_qc_has_all_stations (1 station vs >=3 required), test_zero_unprocessed_files (217 unprocessed vs 0). All 3 regressions stem from the pipeline re-run in commit 7a4b54f.

failing_nodes.message: unprocessed_count is 217 at HEAD, was 0 at last reviewed commit. Pipeline re-run in commit 7a4b54f regressed.
QA/QC report has only 1 station (stanhope) at HEAD, was 5+ at last reviewed commit.

summary: The 3 commits touching src/ introduce good bug fixes (column dedup via coalesce_duplicate_columns, ECCC prefix-stripped column mappings, defensive redundancy.py get) and new code (fwi.py, normalized_loader.py). However, commit 7a4b54f re-ran the pipeline with these fixes and the re-run REGRESSED: unprocessed_count jumped from 0 to 217, QA/QC report dropped from 5+ stations to 1 (stanhope only), and North Rustico early-period data disappeared. The source code changes look correct in isolation, but the pipeline re-run they triggered produced broken output. Ralph needs to investigate why the re-run failed to process most stations — likely the new column mappings changed file recognition or the coalesce logic altered column shapes enough to break station processing.
